title: Vigilance & Harm Taxonomy
copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. Guardian-AV safety margin function d(s) and risk scoring methodology are proprietary NexVigilant methods. Harm taxonomy (Types A-H) follows published pharmacovigilance classification. Theory of Vigilance level mapping is proprietary.
description: Pharmacovigilance vigilance tools: safety margin distance d(s) for composite signal scoring, Guardian-AV risk scoring (0-10), harm classification by type (A-H) per Theory of Vigilance §9, and safety-level-to-ToV mapping. All tools are Rust-native — no network calls required.

tools.name: safety-margin
tools.description: Calculate safety margin distance d(s) — a weighted composite of four disproportionality metrics (PRR, ROR lower CI, IC025, EB05) and case count. d > 1.0 = clear signal; d > 0.5 = probable signal; d > 0.0 = borderline; d <= 0.0 = below threshold. Use after running PRR/ROR/IC/EBGM to synthesize a single decision metric.

parameters.name: ic025
parameters.description: IC lower credibility interval (IC025, from compute-ic)

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Number of case reports for this drug-event pair

d.description: Composite safety distance (positive = above threshold, negative = below)

tools.name: risk-score
tools.description: Compute Guardian-AV risk score (0-10) for a drug-event pair from four disproportionality metrics and case count. Levels: minimal (<2), low (2-4), moderate (4-6), high (6-8), critical (>=8). Combines signal magnitude, CI confidence, Bayesian evidence, information content, and reporting volume.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Adverse event term (MedDRA preferred term recommended)

parameters.name: n
parameters.description: Number of case reports for this drug-event pair

tools.name: harm-types
tools.description: List all 8 harm types (A-H) from the Theory of Vigilance §9. Types derive from three binary attributes: multiplicity (single|multiple) × temporal (acute|chronic) × determinism (deterministic|stochastic) = 2³ = 8 combinations. Returns the complete taxonomy with examples.

taxonomy.description: Array of 8 harm type objects with letter, name, attributes, description, and example

combinatorial_note.description: Derivation note: 2^3 = 8 types from three binary attributes

tools.name: harm-classify
tools.description: Classify an adverse drug reaction into a harm type (A-H) from three binary attributes per Theory of Vigilance §9. Example: single + acute + deterministic = Type A (Augmented, dose-dependent). Use to select monitoring strategy appropriate for the harm mechanism.

parameters.name: multiplicity
parameters.description: Whether the harm requires single or multiple drug exposures: 'single' or 'multiple'

parameters.name: temporal
parameters.description: Onset timing relative to exposure: 'acute' (during/immediately after) or 'chronic' (delayed/cumulative)

parameters.name: determinism
parameters.description: Predictability of the effect: 'deterministic' (dose-dependent, predictable) or 'stochastic' (idiosyncratic, unpredictable)

name.description: Full harm type name (e.g., Type A (Augmented))

description.description: Brief description of the harm mechanism

tools.name: map-to-tov
tools.description: Map a safety level (1-8) to its Theory of Vigilance abstraction tier. Levels: 1=Molecular, 2=Cellular, 3=Tissue, 4=Organ, 5=System, 6=Clinical, 7=Epidemiological, 8=Regulatory. Returns the ToV group (Foundational/Structural/Functional/Population) and description.

safety_level_name.description: Safety level name (e.g., Molecular, Organ, Clinical)

tov_level.description: Theory of Vigilance abstraction level: Foundational | Structural | Functional | Population

tov_group.description: ToV group with level range (e.g., Foundation (1-2))

description.description: Description of what this level covers
